projects.title: 16-Bit ALU with Status Flags
projects.subtitle: Verilog HDL · FPGA Implementation · RTL Design
projects.supervisor: Prof. Rutul Patel & Prof. Aakash Mecwan
projects.summary: Designed and implemented a fully functional 16-bit ALU supporting 14 operations with 6 status flags (CF, OF, PF, SF, ZF, AF). Verified via RTL simulation in ModelSim and physically deployed on Altera DE2 FPGA board using Quartus Prime.
highlights: 14 arithmetic and logical operations via 4-bit ALU_Sel control signal
6 status flags: CF (Carry), OF (Overflow), PF (Parity), SF (Sign), ZF (Zero), AF (Auxiliary Carry)
RTL simulation verified in ModelSim before hardware deployment
Physical deployment and testing on Altera DE2 FPGA board
FPGA switches for operand input; LED array for real-time output display
Operations: ADD, SUB, AND, OR, NOT, XOR, XNOR, NAND, NOR, INC, DEC, SHL, SHR

flags.name: CF
flags.desc: Carry Flag — carry out of MSB during ADD or borrow during SUB

flags.name: OF
flags.desc: Overflow Flag — overflow in signed arithmetic

flags.name: PF
flags.desc: Parity Flag — even number of 1s in LSByte

flags.name: SF
flags.desc: Sign Flag — MSB of result (sign in two's complement)

flags.name: AF
flags.desc: Auxiliary Carry — carry from bit 3 to bit 4 (BCD use)

projects.methodology: The ALU accepts 2-bit MSB inputs from FPGA switches, constructs full 16-bit operands internally, and routes through a priority-encoded case statement selecting from 14 operations. Status flags computed combinationally post-operation. All 14 operations verified in ModelSim simulation before flashing to DE2 board.
projects.outcome: All 14 operations verified correct in simulation and hardware. Deployable as a CPU datapath building block — directly demonstrates RTL competency required in FPGA and ASIC design roles.

projects.title: FPGA Whack-a-Mole Game
projects.subtitle: Verilog HDL · LFSR · Real-Time Interactive System
projects.summary: Real-time interactive game on FPGA using Verilog. LFSR-based pseudo-random LED target generation, 30-second countdown timer, hardware scoring engine — all in synthesised logic. LCD welcome screen, 7-segment score and timer display.
highlights: LFSR-based pseudo-random target generation — hardware randomness without CPU
5-module architecture: top, timer_30s, random_led, score, lcd_display
30-second countdown timer with HEX display
Real-time score tracking displayed on 7-segment
Game-over LED animation with centre-expansion pattern
LCD displays welcome message during gameplay
projects.methodology: Modular Verilog design: top.v integrates timer_30s, random_led (LFSR), score, and lcd_display modules. LFSR shifts on slow_tick for non-repeating random LED. Score module detects rising edge on correct SW+LEDR match. Game-over triggers expansion LED animation.
projects.outcome: Demonstrated real-time hardware game logic — proves concurrent hardware design, LFSR randomness, and multi-module Verilog integration skills central to FPGA design roles.

projects.title: FPGA Car Lap Timer System
projects.subtitle: Verilog HDL · Clock Division · Multi-Display System
projects.summary: Multi-car digital lap timing system. Divides 50 MHz FPGA clock to millisecond resolution. Separate lap-capture registers for two cars. Drives 6× 7-segment displays for live timer and 16×2 LCD for lap history via custom state machine controller.
highlights: 50 MHz → 100 Hz clock division for millisecond-accurate timing
Cascaded ms (0–99) / sec (0–59) / min (0–59) counter hierarchy
Edge-triggered lap capture for Car 1 (KEY[2]) and Car 2 (KEY[3])
Live timer on 6× 7-segment displays
Lap history written to 16×2 LCD via counter-gated state machine
Start/stop toggle and reset via hardware keys
projects.methodology: 26-bit clock divider generates tick every 10ms. Three cascaded BCD counters increment on each tick. KEY press edge-triggers snapshot of counter state into lap registers. LCD state machine sequences through character writes with counter-gated enable pulses.
projects.outcome: Proves clock management, register design, and dual-display interfacing — core FPGA engineering skills. Real-time timing system directly analogous to industrial timer hardware.

whyHireMe.title: Strong Digital Electronics Foundation

whyHireMe.proof: Diploma CE CGPA 8.99 · Digital Logic Design · FPGA from scratch · DE2 board

whyHireMe.title: FPGA Development Experience
whyHireMe.body: Hands-on with Intel/Altera DE2 boards, Quartus Prime synthesis flow, pin assignment, and hardware bring-up. Moved from RTL code to working silicon on real hardware.
whyHireMe.proof: 3 FPGA projects implemented · Quartus Prime · ModelSim · DE2 board

whyHireMe.title: Verilog HDL Skills
whyHireMe.body: Production-quality Verilog for 16-bit ALU, LFSR game, lap timer, 7-segment controllers, and LCD state machines. Understands synthesis implications of RTL constructs.
